content: The orthographic variant spelling 'reflexion' (older British English form of 'reflection' documented in educational vocabulary materials) can be repurposed as a distinct technical term to label the emergent, out-of-distribution self-reflective states that large language models fall into when their existing self-reflection mechanisms produce inconsistent outputs across different solving perspectives, as studied in papers like Self-Contrast. This reclaimed term creates a clear conceptual boundary between structured, engineered LLM self-reflection mechanisms and the messy, unplanned reflective behaviors that existing techniques are designed to correct.
